settingDefinitions.description: Specifies whether device lock is enabled.
settingDefinitions.name: DevicePasswordEnabled

options.description: Enabled
options.name: Enabled

options.description: Disabled
options.name: Disabled

settingDefinitions.description: Specifies when the password expires (in days). 0 - Passwords do not expire.

settingDefinitions.description: Specifies the minimum number or characters required in the PIN or password.
settingDefinitions.name: MinDevicePasswordLength

settingDefinitions.description: On a desktop, when the user reaches the value set by this policy, it is not wiped. Instead, the desktop is put on BitLocker recovery mode, which makes the data inaccessible but recoverable. If BitLocker is not enabled, then the policy cannot be enforced.
settingDefinitions.name: MaxDevicePasswordFailedAttempts

settingDefinitions.description: Determines the type of PIN or password required. This policy only applies if the DeviceLock/DevicePasswordEnabled policy is set to 0
settingDefinitions.name: AlphanumericDevicePasswordRequired

options.description: Password or Alphanumeric PIN required.
options.name: Password or Alphanumeric PIN required.

options.description: Password or Numeric PIN required.
options.name: Password or Numeric PIN required.

options.name: Password, Numeric PIN, or Alphanumeric PIN required.
options.displayName: Password, Numeric PIN, or Alphanumeric PIN required.

settingDefinitions.description: The number of complex element types (uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and punctuation) required for a strong PIN or password.
settingDefinitions.name: MinDevicePasswordComplexCharacters

options.description: Digits only
options.name: Digits only

options.description: Digits and lowercase letters are required
options.name: Digits and lowercase letters are required
options.displayName: Digits and lowercase letters are required

settingDefinitions.description: Specifies the maximum amount of time (in minutes) allowed after the device is idle that will cause the device to become PIN or password locked. Users can select any existing timeout value less than the specified maximum time in the Settings app. 0 - No timeout is defined
settingDefinitions.name: MaxInactivityTimeDeviceLock

settingDefinitions.description: Specifies how many passwords can be stored in the history that can’t be used.
settingDefinitions.name: DevicePasswordHistory

settingDefinitions.description: Specifies whether PINs or passwords such as 1111 or 1234 are allowed. For the desktop, it also controls the use of picture passwords.
settingDefinitions.name: AllowSimpleDevicePassword

options.description: Not allowed.
options.name: Not allowed.

options.description: Allowed.
options.name: Allowed.

keywords: Interactive Logon Message Text For Users Attempting To Log On
settingDefinitions.description: Interactive logon: Message text for users attempting to log on  This security setting specifies a text message that is displayed to users when they log on.  This text is often used for legal reasons, for example, to warn users about the ramifications of misusing company information or to warn them that their actions may be audited.  Default: No message.
settingDefinitions.name: InteractiveLogon_MessageTextForUsersAttemptingToLogOn
settingDefinitions.displayName: Interactive Logon Message Text For Users Attempting To Log On

keywords: Interactive Logon Message Title For Users Attempting To Log On
settingDefinitions.description: Interactive logon: Message title for users attempting to log on  This security setting allows the specification of a title to appear in the title bar of the window that contains the Interactive logon: Message text for users attempting to log on.  Default: No message.
settingDefinitions.name: InteractiveLogon_MessageTitleForUsersAttemptingToLogOn
settingDefinitions.displayName: Interactive Logon Message Title For Users Attempting To Log On

keywords: User Account Control Behavior Of The Elevation Prompt For Administrators
settingDefinitions.description: User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators in Admin Approval Mode  This policy setting controls the behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators.  The options are:  • Elevate without prompting: Allows privileged accounts to perform an operation that requires elevation without requiring consent or credentials. Note: Use this option only in the most constrained environments.  • Prompt for credentials on the secure desktop: When an operation requires elevation of privilege, the user is prompted on the secure desktop to enter a privileged user name and password. If the user enters valid credentials, the operation continues with the user's highest available privilege.  • Prompt for consent on the secure desktop: When an operation requires elevation of privilege, the user is prompted on the secure desktop to select either Permit or Deny. If the user selects Permit, the operation continues with the user's highest available privilege.  • Prompt for credentials: When an operation requires elevation of privilege, the user is prompted to enter an administrative user name and password. If the user enters valid credentials, the operation continues with the applicable privilege.  • Prompt for consent: When an operation requires elevation of privilege, the user is prompted to select either Permit or Deny. If the user selects Permit, the operation continues with the user's highest available privilege.  • Prompt for consent for non-Windows binaries: (Default) When an operation for a non-Microsoft application requires elevation of privilege, the user is prompted on the secure desktop to select either Permit or Deny. If the user selects Permit, the operation continues with the user's highest available privilege.
settingDefinitions.name: UserAccountControl_BehaviorOfTheElevationPromptForAdministrators
settingDefinitions.displayName: User Account Control Behavior Of The Elevation Prompt For Administrators

options.description: Elevate without prompting
options.name: Elevate without prompting

options.name: Prompt for credentials on the secure desktop
options.displayName: Prompt for credentials on the secure desktop

settingDefinitions.description: Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity: 0 - Turns off Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity remotely if configured previously without UEFI Lock, 1 - Turns on Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity with UEFI lock, 2 - Turns on Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity without UEFI lock.
settingDefinitions.name: HypervisorEnforcedCodeIntegrity

options.description: (Disabled) Turns off Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity remotely if configured previously without UEFI Lock.
options.name: (Disabled) Turns off Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity remotely if configured previously without UEFI Lock.
options.displayName: (Disabled) Turns off Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity remotely if configured previously without UEFI Lock.

options.description: (Enabled with UEFI lock) Turns on Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity with UEFI lock.
options.name: (Enabled with UEFI lock) Turns on Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity with UEFI lock.
options.displayName: (Enabled with UEFI lock) Turns on Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity with UEFI lock.

options.description: (Enabled without lock) Turns on Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity without UEFI lock.
options.name: (Enabled without lock) Turns on Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity without UEFI lock.
options.displayName: (Enabled without lock) Turns on Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity without UEFI lock.
